mnemonics: SOM: Self Organizing Maps - See Our Minds (by visualizing high-dim data in 2D)
MCMC: My Car Moves Chaotically (to explore a probability distribution)
MCMC: Must Collect Many Chances (to converge on the true distribution)
keyPoints: SOMs create a low-dimensional (typically 2D) discretized representation of the input space of training samples (a 'map').
MCMC methods are a class of algorithms for sampling from a probability distribution by constructing a Markov chain that has the desired distribution as its equilibrium distribution.
The most common MCMC algorithm is the Metropolis-Hastings algorithm, which uses a proposal distribution to guide a random walk through the parameter space.
commonMistakes: Assuming a SOM is a perfectly precise projection; it is a topology-preserving approximation that can have distortions.
Confusing MCMC with optimization; its goal is not to find a single 'best' point but to characterize an entire distribution.
Using an MCMC chain without checking for convergence, leading to inaccurate sampling from the target distribution.
